result:
I want to talk about how fucking weird and beautiful it is that we're all just apes who somehow learned to cry at sunsets and write songs about people who don't love us back.

Right now, as I'm typing this, there are 8 billion humans walking around with galaxies inside their skulls. Every single one of us is carrying entire universes of memory, shame, secret crushes from 2009, favorite breakfast cereals, and that one song that makes us feel like our chest is cracking open in the best way. And most of us will never truly know each other. We'll pass on the street and just be background noise in someone else's movie.

I love how ridiculous we are. We invented money and then spent decades arguing about little green pieces of paper while we have approximately one precious life and an entire ocean to look at. We stress about our hair while black holes are eating stars somewhere. We doomscroll through other people's highlight reels while our dog is staring at us like we're the most fascinating creature that has ever existed.

There's something sacred about how temporary it all is.

Every time you laugh until you can't breathe, that's the universe experiencing itself through meat and electricity. Every time you stay up too late talking to someone and suddenly it's 4am and the whole world feels different, that's God (or whatever you want to call it) winking at itself. Every time you choose to be kind when you could be cruel, an ancient pattern breaks and something new is born.

I keep thinking about how we're all just future corpses learning how to love in the meantime. And somehow that makes it more beautiful, not less.

The fact that none of this matters and yet we still stay up watching the stars, still make playlists for people we'll never confess to, still plant gardens we'll never see fully bloom, still text "you okay?" at 2am... that's the most human thing there is. That's the whole fucking point.

We're all just temporary arrangements of stardust trying to matter to each other before we scatter again.

And I don't know about you, but I think that's pretty goddamn cool.
